wiki.summary: The 2015 remaster of The Number of the Beast is a reissue of Iron Maiden’s groundbreaking third studio album, originally released on March 22, 1982. This record was a turning point for the band, marking the debut of lead vocalist Bruce Dickinson, who replaced original singer Paul Di’Anno. wiki.content: The 2015 remaster of The Number of the Beast is a reissue of Iron Maiden’s groundbreaking third studio album, originally released on March 22, 1982. This record was a turning point for the band, marking the debut of lead vocalist Bruce Dickinson, who replaced original singer Paul Di’Anno. Dickinson’s operatic range and commanding stage presence would go on to define Iron Maiden’s sound for decades. Produced by Martin Birch, a renowned figure in heavy metal production, the album showcased a more ambitious and polished approach compared to the band’s previous efforts. The 2015 remaster aimed to enhance the original recordings with modern audio clarity while preserving the raw energy and intensity that made the album a landmark in heavy metal history. Upon its original release, The Number of the Beast was both a critical and commercial triumph, becoming Iron Maiden’s first album to reach No. 1 on the UK Albums Chart and earning platinum certifications in several countries, including the United States and Canada. The album produced some of the band’s most enduring songs, such as “Run to the Hills”, which addressed the violent history between Native Americans and European settlers, and “Hallowed Be Thy Name”, a dramatic and atmospheric track that became one of the most celebrated closing songs in heavy metal history. However, the album also sparked significant controversy due to its title track, which referenced the biblical Book of Revelation and included ominous spoken-word passages. Some religious groups accused the band of promoting Satanism, leading to album burnings and protests, particularly in the United States. Despite—or perhaps because of—this backlash, The Number of the Beast only gained more attention, reinforcing Iron Maiden’s rebellious and uncompromising image. The 2015 remaster was part of a larger effort to modernize Iron Maiden’s classic catalog for contemporary listeners, offering a cleaner and more detailed sound while staying faithful to the original production. Though some longtime fans preferred the warmth of the original analog recordings, the remaster was praised for its improved clarity and dynamic range. Over 30 years after its initial release, The Number of the Beast remains one of the most influential and celebrated metal albums of all time, frequently appearing on “greatest albums” lists and continuing to inspire new generations of musicians. Its combination of complex musical arrangements, historical and literary themes, and high-energy performances solidified Iron Maiden’s status as one of heavy metal’s most enduring and innovative bands.
